reviews.review_description: I love this app. Fun,relaxing and educational.

This is brilliant im using a note 8 and i can say ive had no issues with anything. This really puts you inside the iss and the space walk just wow what an amazing effort you have put into this and i cant think for one moment why its free because i would have paid for this.
Neil Armstrong wouldn’t have bothered going to Astronaut school if he’d seen this first. Wow.
Great experience. A little tough to get the hang of but very cool concept and graphics. Super cool to float around in space.
Its just that good.

Sorry. I wanted so bad to play this. I have the newest Gear with the Oculus controller with a new Note 8 and this is simply uncontrollable. I have no idea how other reviewers were able to use this. Everytime I touch the touchpad I get rotated 90 degrees. Its not a learning curve thing, its a breath on the controller and get rotated 90 degrees thing. 
I have even had others try this and nobody can move 2 feet without getting jostled 90 degrees in every direction. I have yet to see anyone follow the arrows down ONE STRAIGHT hallway succesfully. After days I am convinced its a bad control scheme mixed with hardware/software issues. Its a shame to see what appears to be a beautiful app destroyed by terrible control issues. No joke, I am a little convinced this app is a practical joke. If so, you guys got me!
 If I could even do the training mission I would be happy. The limited settings do nothing to fix the problem. Lastly, I'm an experienced pilot, who's hobby is orbital mechanics, unstable aircraft and flight physics... 
I can promise you one thing. It is easier to learn to fly from scratch, be accepted into NASA, pass the astronaut program, launch into space, than it is to move 2 feet in this thing

The only thing that I would like is  To travel to different solar systems and planets

T
This app is better than alot of paid apps similar to it. Unfortunately I dont have the best VR legs and it makes me a bit motion sick.

I am absolutely stunned, the experience is amazing!
A fantastic app, I could float out there in space for ever. Fully imersive experience, can't wait to see any future developments on this amazing theme. As for control, on the Oculus Go it just takes a bit of practice.
Can we have this app on the Oculus Quest please ?

reviews.review_title: The pad controls on the Go are abysmal
reviews.review_description: These controls are absolutely ABYSMAL on the Go. As of now this game is completely unplayable for me. The response on this stupid touch pad is well... unresponsive! I'm upset because I really wanted to enjoy this experience. But it turned in to a nightmare and made me ill.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback Sininside,

We do understand that at some point users are getting sick in VR. This is normal for this type of tech. For the controls, we are continuously reinventing ourselves to make our customers comfortable in our experiences. Please be patient with us as we continue to build on our applications taking into account feedback from our user base. Don't be afraid to try our other experiences! 

Thanks,

reviews.review_description: It's an absolutely amazing experience if you love space as much as me but the mechanics of the game are atrocious you need to make it feel more human like add if you're actually grabbing the things other than just manipulating them because currently it feels like a documentary that is annoying to continue with the story

developer_response.body: Thanks for the constructive feedback joshuafm. We are continuously trying to reinvent ourselves through feedback such as yours. Please feel free to check out our other experiences!

reviews.review_description: This was the first VR experience to make me sick to my stomach. The navigation and physics of weightlessness is just too much. Also, the astronaut body you’re occupying doesn’t take up any space, so nothing prevents you from bumping into a wall. So you end up looking up from the floor in a way that would be physically impossible, which messed with me. This would be better if I were just suspended in the center of the corridor, but floating until bumping into something or grabbing something was awful. It’s a beautiful game otherwise but the nauseating physics make it a no-go for me.
This feels so realistic and I actually felt nauseous at one point.  I have a cousin who spent time on the ISS, so it’s pretty cool to get a glimpse of what he lived.

This is among the first 10 or so apps that I tried since purchasing my Gear VR, and my mind is blown. This is by far the most amazing piece of software I have had a chance to play with. At one point I forgot I was in VR, and instinctively reached out to grab a bulkhead with my hand. Reviewers like Fang333333 have no idea what they've just missed - if you take the time to listen and follow instructions in the training segment, you'll be moving around the zero-g environment with ease (I did, and I do). Operating the docking arm was a challenge the first time I tried it, but the second time I listened to the instructions once more and I nailed it.
Us grownups tend to think we can observe a skill once and be masters on the first go. But take the time to learn the controls and you'll have the reward of the most life-altering experience you can have while spending an evening at home

The visual is awesome. You truly feel like you are floating inside the space station. We can grab some boxes and throw them around that’s pretty cool. The problem is I can not control when I try to move forward . It just rotate 90 all the time. It was frustrating. You just want to move in a straight line and stop when you want. I could not stop. This app has the potential to become awesome but for now the control is terrible. Hopefully the developers can fixed this bug. I have an oculus go and it definitely does not work properly on this device

developer_response.body: We're very sorry to hear that you're having issues with Mission ISS on Oculus Go schretien777. We tried to simulate the best way we could to justify users actually experiencing 0 gravity inside the ISS. We're hoping you'll give us another chance with some of our other experiences out on the market right now. We are working on better control schemes for our future products. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: Great experience and the controls are ok, you just need to get used to it. Thank you!

I downloaded it on my go and opened it gave me a black scree closed it and tried again black screen... Tried for a third time I could hear music but still black screen and the last time it crashed my headset I think because I couldn't get it turned off for a long time no sound couldn't get to the home screen nothing. I had to hold the off button down for over 30 seconds just to turn it off so I uninstalled it.
That's wonderful !

The touchpad controls both your position and rotation, by pressing down and swiping respectively. This causes the issue of when trying to move you accidentally start rotating 90 degrees. Would be much better if a physical button press like the trigger combined with swiping could control the rotation so you don’t get accidental swipes.

developer_response.body: Thanks for reaching out to us gigabyte898. Please be patient as we continue to find ways for our users to experience our applications in the most comfortable ways possible. Don't hesitate to check out our other experiences in the meantime!

reviews.review_description: I have being trying to dowoad this video but with no luck
